entry.title: Taipei Times: 10 Jul, 2022

entry.content:

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 9 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-09 22:52PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘Significant improvement’ in young people’s willingness to join Hong Kong police, chief says, with growing interest at recruitment events
  • Hong Kong civil servant, 36, arrested over suspicious white powder sent to High Court worker
  • Hong Kong doesn’t need teachers who are against national education: John Lee
  • Hong Kong proposes 2-device cap on car dashboards, with HK$2,000 fine for offenders in safety push

  • Coronavirus: should Hong Kong be concerned about global rise in BA.4 and BA.5 cases? Here’s what we know about more transmissive Omicron subvariants

  • South China Sea: US-led Southeast Asian strategy to contain Beijing would ‘complicate’ disputes, Chinese experts warn

  • Four injured in knife attack at Shanghai hospital
  • China, EU spar over way forward, as human rights, Russia and trade dominate farewell event for Brussels envoy
  • Australian and Chinese foreign ministers meet as ‘first step’ in efforts to restore relations
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping conveys ‘deep regret’ over sudden death of ex-Japan leader Shinzo Abe
  • China inflation: rise in demand due to eased Covid-19 curbs forecast to drive up consumer prices
  • New US Space Force spy agency points to earthly rivalries being played out in orbit
  • On top of censorship, Chinese writers now have to contend with attacks from patriotic trolls, and that’s worrying
  • Japan votes as polls open in shadow of ex-prime minister Shinzo Abe’s assassination
  • India fines Amnesty International US$8 million after funding probe
  • Shinzo Abe assassination: From Singapore to India, tributes flow for ardent champion of ‘free and open Indo-Pacific’
  • Sri Lanka’s president and prime minister to resign following day of violent protests
  • Indonesia could issue a fatwa that medical marijuana is halal – thanks to a mother’s love
  • ‘I feel responsible’ for Shinzo Abe shooting, police chief says as Japan struggles to come to terms with ex-PM’s death
  • Shinzo Abe’s shooter Tetsuya Yamagami initially planned to attack leader of Japan religious group that bankrupted his mum: police
  • ‘Inflation jihad’, cash handouts: Can Singapore, Malaysia and other Asian countries soften the blow of rising prices?
  • Upholding common law, tackling vested interests: Xi’s vision of Hong Kong strikes a chord
  • Moscow-born Elena Rybakina powers past Ons Jabeur to Wimbledon singles title
  • Techno party with Love Parade founder hits Berlin’s streets
  • Three more UK Tory MPs unveil bid to replace Boris Johnson
  • Volodymyr Zelensky sacks Ukraine’s envoy to Germany, among other ambassadors
  • Date-rape drugs shock at German chancellor’s summer party
  • Russia’s aim to ‘raise true hell’ with deadly shelling overnight, war in spotlight at G20 meeting
  • Britons may hate Boris Johnson but he is still popular in Ukraine
  • WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange ‘will die’ if extradited to US, Australian journalist John Pilger says
  • The Boris Johnson era is ending. What about Westminster sleaze culture?
  • Ukraine says no power, water in Russian-held Sievierodonetsk, bodies rotting in flats
  • China’s largest carmaker sees NEV deliveries almost double in June as supply chain woes ease
  • Breaking up HSBC: Ping An’s push to extract value from bank’s split fails to excite shareholders as unorthodox campaign enters its third month
  • The Nothing Phone 1 is Carl Pei’s first move in trying to shake up the smartphone since leaving OnePlus. Will it work?
  • How Running Out of Time, starring Andy Lau and Lau Ching-wan, helped shape the ‘Johnnie To Cinematic Universe’
  • Sex or your phone – which would you rather go without? Expert relationship advice for those who said the former
  • Casanova: world’s greatest lover or debauched sexual predator? Harvard professor puts legendary libertine’s conquests in context
  • The fashion of South Korea’s first lady Kim Keon-hee makes her unusual – she’s unlike any of the women that came before her
  • Jung Jin-young, Cho Yi-hyun set for Summer Vacation; Sung Joon joins Tell Me It’s Love – K-drama casting latest
  • Why close a popular restaurant? A chef-owner in Hong Kong says it’s sad but to grow you must move on
  • From Sin City to Lion City, nicknames for world cities and how they were coined
  • What are China’s ‘sponge cities’? The urban flooding solution that’s being trialed across the country to counter an increasingly deadly problem
  • Why good dental hygiene is essential to reduce inflammation and slow the onset of chronic diseases and ‘inflammaging’
  • 14 most extravagant celebrity private jets: from Kim Kardashian’s cashmere-clad Kim Air, and Jay-Z and Beyoncé’s Bombardier Challenger, to tech billionaires Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos’ Gulfstream G550s
  • Who is Sheryl Sandberg, Mark Zuckerberg’s right-hand woman? Meta’s COO of 14 years is stepping down from the company – but keeps a mighty net worth of US$1.5 billion
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

  • Mick Herron: ‘I’m interested in incompetence, things going wrong’
  • ‘Jokes are strategic’: how Mykolaiv’s leader uses humour to resist Putin
  • When it comes to banter, men are in their element. But that is no foundation for lasting friendship

entry.title: Korea Herald: 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • S. Korea's new COVID-19 cases back above 20,000 in 1 1/2 months

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 9 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Shinzo Abe Is Assassinated With a Handmade Gun, Shocking a Nation
  • Assassination Shocks a Nearly Gun-Free Japan
  • U.S. Economy Added 372,000 Jobs in June, Defying Slowdown Fears
